factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in utility and renewable energy sectors.

evidence.text: Revenues increased 46.7%, or $1.26 billion, to $3.97 billion as compared to consolidated revenues of $2.70 billion for the three months ended March 31, 2021; Operating income increased 3.3%, or $3.8 million, to $117.5 million as compared to $113.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021; EBITDA increased 52.3%, or $105.0 million, to $305.8 million, as compared to $200.8 million for the three months ended March 31, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows significant revenue and EBITDA growth, indicating strong demand acceleration.

evidence.text: Remaining performance obligations increased 16.1%, or $947.2 million, to $6.84 billion as of March 31, 2022 as compared to $5.90 billion as of December 31, 2021; Total backlog (a non-GAAP financial measure) increased 6.1%, or $1.18 billion, to $20.5 billion as of March 31, 2022, as compared to $19.27 billion as of December 31, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Both remaining performance obligations and total backlog have increased, signaling growing demand.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting, with a focus on higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Revenues increased by 27.6% to $2.14 billion, as compared to $1.68 billion; Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Revenues increased by 128.0% to $875.6 million, as compared to $384.1 million; Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Revenues increased by 47.8% to $951.2 million, as compared to $643.5 million.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue growth across all segments, particularly in higher-margin renewable energy, suggests a favorable shift.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the Electric Power segment.

evidence.text: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Revenues increased by 27.6% to $2.14 billion, as compared to $1.68 billion; Operating income increased by 32.3% to $203.4 million, as compared to $153.7 million.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong organic growth in the Electric Power segment with significant revenue and operating income increases.

factors.summary: End markets in renewable energy and underground utilities are growing fastest.

evidence.text: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Revenues increased by 128.0% to $875.6 million, as compared to $384.1 million; Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Revenues increased by 47.8% to $951.2 million, as compared to $643.5 million.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Rapid growth in renewable energy and underground utility segments indicates strong end-market momentum.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding in some segments but compressing in others.

evidence.text: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Operating margin increased to 9.5%, as compared to 9.2%; Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Operating margin decreased to 8.0%, as compared to 11.8%; Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Operating margin increased to 5.1%, as compared to 1.4%.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed margin performance across segments, with some improving and others declining, results in a neutral assessment.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the Underground segment.

evidence.text: Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Operating income and operating margin increased in the three months ended March 31, 2022 due to the increase in revenues, which contributed to higher levels of fixed cost absorption. Also contributing to the increases were improved performance across the segment, including in our industrial services operations due to the reduced negative impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic as compared to the same period in 2021, as well as favorable project timing and project mix.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved productivity in the Underground segment, driven by higher revenues and better performance post-pandemic.

factors.summary: Construction infrastructure spending is growing, particularly in utility and renewable energy sectors.

evidence.text: The performance of our business generally depends on our ability to obtain contracts with customers and to effectively deliver the services provided under those contracts; Revenues increased 46.7%, or $1.26 billion, to $3.97 billion as compared to consolidated revenues of $2.70 billion for the three months ended March 31, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant revenue growth and increased infrastructure spending in utility and renewable sectors.
